contract.name: v2/download/status.md
contract.description: Retrieves the status of a USAspending CSV download job identified by its file_name.

file_name.description: Exact download job filename returned by a download initiation endpoint.
file_name.constraints: Required query parameter. Blank values are rejected as missing (400 detail). Unknown names return 404 detail. Parameter name is case-sensitive, and if repeated the endpoint uses the last occurrence.

status.description: Download job state reported by the server; observed values: running, finished.

message.description: Nullable human-readable message describing failures. Observed as null while jobs are running or finished.

file_name.description: Filename of the generated ZIP archive.

file_url.description: Absolute HTTPS URL for the generated archive.

total_size.description: Estimated file size in kilobytes. Null while running; populated upon completion.

total_columns.description: Number of columns in generated CSVs. May report interim counts while status is running.

total_rows.description: Number of rows in generated CSVs. May report interim counts while status is running.

seconds_elapsed.description: Elapsed processing time in seconds, encoded as a string.

detail.description: Error detail string returned for invalid or unknown file_name requests (400/404 responses).

body.detail: Download job with filename foo does not exist.
Missing one or more required query parameters: file_name

quirks: Error responses return only a detail string and omit the status payload.
Query parameter names are case-sensitive; misspellings such as file_Name are treated as missing.
When file_name appears multiple times the endpoint uses the last value in the query string.
file_url values are absolute HTTPS links despite documentation showing relative paths.
total_rows and total_columns can update between running polls, so interim totals are not final.
risks: Download job filenames may expire or be unknown, leading to frequent 404 detail responses.
Clients must handle error responses that only contain a detail string rather than the documented status payload.
Partial metrics in running responses can change; treating them as final can produce inconsistent reporting.
gaps: Failed and ready statuses were not observed; error payloads and message content for those states are unknown.

body.detail: Download job with filename foo does not exist.
Missing one or more required query parameters: file_name
Missing one or more required query parameters: file_name

mismatches: Docs describe only a 200 response with job metadata, but the live API returns 404 with a detail string when the filename does not exist.
Docs show file_url as a relative path, while production responses return absolute HTTPS URLs.
Docs state total_rows and total_columns remain null until completion, yet running responses contained numeric values.
gaps: Failed and ready statuses were not observed; their payload structure and message usage remain undocumented.
risks: Download job filenames may expire or be unknown, leading to frequent 404 detail responses.
Clients must handle error responses that only contain a detail string rather than the documented status payload.
Partial metrics in running responses can change; treating them as final can produce inconsistent reporting.
